samples.gvd:
module RAM
#(
    parameter WIDTH = 6,
    parameter DEPTH = 8
)
(
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    input write_en,
    input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] write_addr,
    input [WIDTH-1:0] write_data,
    input read_en,
    input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] read_addr,
    output [WIDTH-1:0] read_data
);
    reg [WIDTH-1:0] RAM [2**WIDTH-1:0];
    integer i;
    assign read_data = read_en ? RAM[read_addr] : 0;
    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if (!rst_n) begin
            for (i = 0; i < 2**WIDTH; i = i + 1) begin
                RAM[i] <= 0;
            end
        end
        else if (write_en) begin
            RAM[write_addr] <= write_data;
        end
    end
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
Implement a dual-port RAM with a depth of 8 and a bit width of 6 bits, with all data initialized to 000000. It has two groups of ports, respectively for reading data and writing data, and read and write operations can be carried out at the same time. When the read_en signal is 1, the read_data of the corresponding position is read through the read_addr signal and output; When the write_en signal is 1, data is written to the corresponding position through the write_addr signal and write-data signal.. Clock signal used for synchronous operation. Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Write enable signal to initiate a write operation. Address for the write operation. Data to be written to the RAM. Read enable signal to initiate a read operation. Address for the read operation. Output signal representing the data read from the RAM. Width of the data. Depth of the RAM.

Define a RAM array with a depth of 8 and a width of 6 bits, and initialize all data to 000000.

reg [5:0] RAM [0:8-1];

Trigger the write operation on the positive edge of the clock signal or the negative edge of the reset signal.

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)

Reset the RAM array.

if (!rst_n) RAM[0] <= 6'b000000; RAM[1] <= 6'b000000; RAM[2] <= 6'b000000; RAM[3] <= 6'b000000; RAM[4] <= 6'b000000; RAM[5] <= 6'b000000; RAM[6] <= 6'b000000; RAM[7] <= 6'b000000;

Write data to the RAM array when the write enable signal is 1.

if (write_en) RAM[write_addr] <= write_data;

Trigger the read operation on the positive edge of the clock signal or the negative edge of the reset signal.

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)

Reset the read data.

if (!rst_n) read_data <= 6'b000000;

Read data from the RAM array when the read enable signal is 1.

if (read_en) read_data <= RAM[read_addr];

Clear the read data when the read enable signal is not 1.

else read_data <= 6'b000000;
